intro: Teutliopsis hastata, also known as Teutliopsis or Wild Teutliopsis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its reported health benefits of reducing pain, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Teutliopsis hastata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
